title: Hempnall Mosquito Memorial
crew_summary: 2 killed: Flt Lt. John Gordon, DFC and Flg Off. Ralph Gamble Hayes, DFC

external_narratives.summary: Detailed page on Flt Lt John Gordon DFC and his navigator Flg Off Ralph Gamble Hayes DFC of 105 Squadron. On 5 November 1943, 24 Mosquitoes were detailed to attack chemical works at Leverkusen, with DZ587 (coded GB-B) taking off at 17:29. The aircraft was observed circling Hardwick Airfield throwing out distress flares before crashing between the airfield and Hempnall village.
key_details: Target was chemical works at Leverkusen
Aircraft seen circling Hardwick Airfield firing distress flares before crash

external_narratives.summary: The Imperial War Museum holds photographic records of 105 Squadron Mosquito operations from Marham during 1942-1945, providing context for the unit's low-level precision bombing campaigns.
key_details: IWM holds photographic records of 105 Squadron operations
